cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Kana Research: 70% of Enterprises Run AI Agents in Marketing But Governance Ownership Is Unclear
stories.plain: A survey of 225 senior enterprise leaders finds agentic AI adoption has outpaced governance: most companies already run marketing agents, but nobody clearly owns accountability for them. This is a landmark signal that the industry's next big battle is not deployment — it is control, liability, and oversight frameworks.

stories.head: Acxiom Launches Digital Recognition to Link Online and Offline Identity for Omnichannel Personalization
stories.plain: Acxiom's expanded Digital Recognition ties live digital signals to its offline identity graph, with early adopters reporting a 50% lift in website visitor recognition. Better identity resolution directly improves how CDPs and personalization engines match and target real individuals across channels — a foundational capability upgrade.

stories.head: Cision Adds AI Search Visibility Dashboard to CisionOne, Tracking Brand Presence in ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude
stories.plain: Cision now monitors how brands are cited inside major LLM answer engines and surfaces that data alongside traditional media metrics. Generative engine optimization is becoming a standard PR requirement, and this is one of the first enterprise-grade tools to measure it at scale — making it a buying signal for CMOs.

stories.head: HubSpot CMO Kipp Bodnar on Brand Discovery When AI Answers Replace Search Results
stories.plain: HubSpot's CMO argues that attribution models built for click-based search are blind to AI-driven discovery, where prospects find solutions inside LLM answers before ever visiting a website. The interview reframes how marketers must think about top-of-funnel visibility and measurement strategy going forward.

stories.head: Banco do Brasil Deploys Agentic AI Copilot Across Core Banking Workflows for Personalized Customer Engagement
stories.plain: One of Latin America's largest banks has embedded an agentic AI copilot into live relationship-banking workflows, enabling staff to deliver compliant, personalized service at scale. This shows agentic AI moving beyond marketing pilots into regulated, high-stakes enterprise operations — a sign of the technology's maturity and governance requirements.

stories.head: Kana Research: 70% of Enterprises Run AI Agents in Marketing, But Ownership Remains Unclear
stories.plain: A survey of 225 senior marketing, data, and AI leaders at large U.S. enterprises finds that agentic AI adoption has outpaced governance: seven in ten already run AI agents in marketing, yet no one clearly owns them. This gap between deployment speed and accountability structures is the defining risk question for anyone buying or building marketing AI right now.

stories.head: Acxiom Launches Digital Recognition to Link Online and Offline Identity for Omnichannel Personalization
stories.plain: Acxiom's expanded Digital Recognition solution ties anonymous digital signals to its offline identity graph, with top brands reporting a 50% lift in site-visitor recognition. That improvement in identity resolution directly powers real-time personalization and closes the online-to-offline measurement gap that has long frustrated omnichannel marketers.

stories.head: Cision Adds AI Search Visibility Dashboard to CisionOne, Tracking Brand Presence in ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude
stories.plain: Cision now pulls signals from major LLMs directly into its media intelligence platform, giving PR and marketing teams a single dashboard to monitor how their brand appears inside AI-generated answers. As more buyers start discovery in AI chat rather than search engines, this kind of GEO measurement is becoming a basic requirement.

stories.head: HubSpot CMO Kipp Bodnar on Brand Discovery When AI Search Replaces Traditional Attribution
stories.plain: HubSpot's CMO explains how AI-driven discovery is breaking conventional attribution models, leaving marketers blind to the moments that matter most to future customers. The interview gives practical framing for why brand strategy, content authority, and GEO need to move up the priority list alongside performance marketing.

stories.head: AI Adoption Hits 90% in CX But Organizations Split on Architecture and Governance
stories.plain: A broad CX study finds near-universal AI adoption but sharp disagreement on how to build it: centralized vs. distributed architectures, who governs automated decisions, and how to demonstrate ROI while maintaining customer trust. These structural choices are shaping which vendors get selected and which deployments stall.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Agents Are Everywhere — But Ownership Is Unresolved
body: Kana research finds 70% of enterprises already run AI agents in marketing, yet accountability for those agents sits in no clear organizational home. BMC and Medallia are racing to layer governance and guardrails onto agent deployments, and Banco do Brasil is embedding agentic AI directly into core banking workflows — signalling that adoption has far outpaced the internal structures meant to manage it.

The practical consequence is a governance gap that will force a decision: does the AI agent belong to IT, Marketing, or a new hybrid function? Firms that answer this question first will move faster & with less risk than those still debating it during the next budget cycle.
ev: Agile Brand · Banco do Brasil agentic AI

themes.head: AI Search Visibility Rewrites Brand Discovery
body: Cision has added AI search visibility tracking to CisionOne so PR teams can monitor how brands appear inside AI-generated answers — a category that did not exist two years ago. HubSpot CMO Kipp Bodnar & the MarTech piece on AI search trust both make the same point: the rules of brand discovery have fundamentally changed now that AI intermediaries, not search-engine result pages, answer most queries.

Trust signals — authoritative citations, structured data, consistent entity information — have become the new SEO. Marketers who still measure success only by traditional keyword rankings are optimising for a channel that is rapidly losing primacy in the discovery funnel.
ev: Agile Brand · HubSpot CMO on AI search

themes.head: The CMS Becomes the AI Operating System for Brands
body: MarTech's analysis argues that the content management system is evolving into the central orchestration layer through which brands deploy AI — generating, personalising, & distributing content at scale without rebuilding infrastructure from scratch. This reframes the CMS from a publishing tool into a strategic platform that mediates every AI-driven brand interaction.

The implication for buyers is significant: CMS vendor selection is now effectively AI-stack selection. Teams evaluating platforms need to ask not just about content workflows but about model integration, data governance, & the agent-readiness questions MarTech's vendor audit guide flags explicitly.

themes.head: Identity Resolution Closes the Omnichannel Gap
body: Acxiom's Digital Recognition launch connects online behavioural data to offline identity records, aiming to give brands a single persistent view of each customer across every channel. Meanwhile Radial's research ties B2B fulfilment quality directly to omnichannel brand success, and MarTech's digital video analysis shows intensifying competition for cross-screen attention — all pointing to identity resolution as the linchpin of any serious personalisation strategy.

With third-party cookies effectively gone & walled gardens fragmenting audience data, brands that cannot stitch together their own first-party identity graph will be structurally disadvantaged in targeting, measurement, & retention — no matter how sophisticated their AI models become.

themes.head: AI Raises an Urgent Talent & Skill Debt
body: MarTech's piece on training asks a pointed question: if AI handles execution, where do junior marketers learn the craft? At the same time, AI adoption in CX has hit 90% — yet deployment paths are diverging sharply between teams that genuinely upskill people & those that simply automate away tasks. Medallia's push for frontline-ready AI and the rise of vibe coding both suggest organisations are betting on AI assistance before humans have the judgment to oversee it well.

The risk is compounding: firms that skip the skill-building phase save money short-term but produce teams unable to audit, redirect, or recover from AI errors. Structuring AI tools so they teach rather than merely replace is the strategic differentiator that separates sustainable adoption from fragile automation.
ev: MarTech · AI adoption hits 90% in CX

month.takeaway: Every major move this month — from agent governance to identity resolution to CMS evolution — points to the same inflection: the infrastructure decisions brands make right now will determine whether AI becomes a durable competitive advantage or an ungoverned liability.
